login.login_desc: Login to your Arabic College portal

about.title: About the College
about.subtitle: Rooted in Classical Tradition, Committed to Contemporary Academic Standards
about.history_p1: Founded in 2012, the Arabic College of Sharia and Linguistic Sciences was established to address the growing need for high-caliber, academically structured education in classical Islamic subjects. Rather than relying on informal study circles, our founders designed a comprehensive university-level curriculum.
about.history_p2: Today, our campus serves as a leading institution in Sharia jurisprudence, Arabic morphology and syntax, and Hadith criticism. We blend time-tested textual learning of classical Islamic manuals with structured semester formats, coursework grading, and credit-hour criteria.
about.mission_desc: To cultivate accomplished scholars and researchers in Sharia sciences and classical Arabic literature, empowering them with critical thinking, research skills, and authentic traditional insights to guide the contemporary community.
about.vision_desc: To be the premier global center of higher learning for traditional Sharia sciences and Arabic linguistic research, recognized for combining classical depth with academic integrity.
about.authenticity_desc: Upholding chains of narration and classical understandings of early Sharia scholars.
about.critical_thinking_desc: Equipping students with comparative analysis methodologies in comparative Fiqh.
about.relevance_desc: Bridging traditional texts to answer modern, legal, and financial queries.

admissions.title: Admissions Info
admissions.subtitle: Begin your academic path at the Arabic College
admissions.req_desc: Arabic College welcomes applicants from all backgrounds who demonstrate a commitment to rigorous academic work and respect for classical academic traditions.
admissions.eligibility_1: High School Diploma or equivalent certificate with a minimum grade point average (GPA) of 80%.
admissions.eligibility_2: Basic proficiency in the Arabic language (written and spoken) is required for Sharia and Hadith tracks.
admissions.eligibility_3: Good conduct certificate from the applicant's prior educational institution.
admissions.eligibility_4: Passing the college's standard Arabic proficiency entrance exam.
admissions.doc_2: Copy of National ID card or Passport
admissions.cal_desc_1: Submit your personal parameters, phone, and upload transcript documents via the applicant portal.
admissions.cal_desc_2: Conducted online and on-campus for registered applicants.
admissions.cal_desc_3: Approved applicants will be notified and sent tuition invoices.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.subtitle: Get in touch with the Arabic College administration team
contact.info_desc: For immediate registrar help, syllabus questions, or general campus guidance, reach out to us directly:
contact.msg_success_desc: Thank you. We have successfully received your query parameters. Our registrar administration team will review and reply to {{email}} shortly.

downloads.title: Downloads Center
downloads.subtitle: Access official prospectus documents, registration templates, and syllabus brochures
downloads.notice: Documents are distributed in standard PDF format. If you require specialized print brochures, please contact the registrar team.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Find answers to common questions about admissions, programs, and portals
faq.q1: When do registrations for new applicants open?
faq.a1: Admissions registrations typically open in late June and close on July 31st. All documents including high school transcripts and ID copies must be uploaded during this period.
faq.q2: How does the placement entrance exam work?
faq.a2: After submitting your application online, qualified applicants will sit for an Arabic language placement entrance exam in mid-August. This helps determine your suitability for advanced classical tracks.
faq.q3: What language are classes taught in?
faq.a3: All major modules (Islamic Sharia law, Hadith chain critiques, Arabic literature) are taught entirely in classical Arabic. General utility courses or linguistic tools might use translated guides, but core instructions are in Arabic.
faq.a4: Yes. All undergraduate degree programs (Bachelor of Sharia, Bachelor of Arabic Language, and Bachelor of Hadith Sciences) are fully accredited by national educational regulatory boards.
faq.q5: How do I pay my semester tuition invoice fees?
faq.a5: Student invoices are generated at the start of each semester. You can log in to your student portal dashboard, check invoices, and make payments using card, bank transfer, or check.
faq.q6: What should I do if I didn't receive my email verification OTP?
faq.a6: Check your spam/junk folder. Alternatively, you can click "Resend OTP" on the verification screen. Local testing environments write these emails directly to the system logs.

news.title: News & Events
news.subtitle: Stay up to date with the latest activities, timetables, and academic bulletins
news.ann_content_1: Applications are now being accepted for B-Sharia, B-Arabic, and B-Hadith programs for the academic year 2026/2027. Apply online through our admissions portal.
news.ann_content_2: We are pleased to introduce our digital catalog system. Students can now search books, renew loans, and inspect reference availability online.
